name: Abaho Katabarwa
bio: I'm a Ugandan-American engineer and founder with a deep interest in building products that make powerful technology accessible — not just to developers, but to everyone.

My career spans embedded systems at Bowers & Wilkins (shipping real-time audio infrastructure under sub-10-microsecond latency budgets), conversational AI tooling at Microsoft Semantic Machines, and the evaluation pipeline powering Microsoft Copilot.
Today I'm building App Maker — a low-to-no-code agentic workflow platform that lets non-technical users design, deploy, and monitor complex AI workflows without writing a single line of code.
Outside of engineering, I'm a long-distance runner chasing my next mile personal record.
heroSubtitle: Systems engineer and founder building AI-powered tools that make complex technology accessible to everyone. Previously at Microsoft, Stanford, and Brown.
